mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that translates into higher corporate profit margins, yielding positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when filtered to this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3% triggers immediate market repricing for accelerated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls sustain household income growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day equity returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with accelerating retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns over the next month through improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the joint signal produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production alongside rising unemployment signal an imminent economic contraction, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where both conditions are met, or the trigger fails to separate bull and bear outcomes with accuracy above 58%.
